answer:
~$0 federal tax on gain. Likely full exclusion.

Why: Stock acquired 2016 = after Sept 27, 2010 → 100% QSBS exclusion under §1202. Held >5 years ✓. $8M gain under cap (greater of $10M or 10× basis) ✓. With 100% exclusion, excluded gain also exempt from AMT preference and 3.8% NIIT. So federal owed on this gain: zero.

Caveats — verify before selling:

- Must be original issuance from corporation (not bought from another holder).
- Corp gross assets ≤ $50M at issuance; active qualified business (not services, finance, hospitality, etc.) during substantially all holding period.
- Redemptions by company near your issuance can disqualify.
- Options/RSUs: 5-year clock starts at exercise/settlement, not grant — 2016 "acquired" must mean shares issued then.
- 2025 law changes (higher $15M cap, tiered exclusion) apply only to stock acquired after July 4, 2025 — irrelevant to you.
- State tax separate: CA, PA, others don't follow §1202 — could owe big state tax. Check your state.

Recommendation: get QSBS attestation letter from company + CPA sign-off before sale. Stakes = ~$1.9M+ federal tax if disqualified.
